cataloging_subject.label: Cursor Mundi
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The catalog subject is the historical Morris edited compilation represented by the pinned XML. It centers on anonymous Middle English Cursor Mundi but also contains editorial tables, parallel witness streams, additions, appendices, and an incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals. The fourteen DIV2 groups are not fourteen independent works, and the two confirmed conceptual components are not encoded as an exhaustive item total.

notes: A concise discovery title for the historical edited compilation; it does not erase the incomplete Cato appendix.

summaries.value: A historical Richard Morris edited compilation centered on the anonymous Middle English Cursor Mundi, a Northern English universal chronicle and didactic religious poem in verse. The source presents parallel manuscript streams, editorial tables, additions, prayers, appendices, and an incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals. Its fourteen XML DIV2 groups are modeled as a partial structural inventory rather than fourteen independent works.

Two conceptual components are confirmed: Cursor Mundi, counted once despite parallel versions, and the incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals appendix. The independent-work status of named Göttingen additions, prayers, Penance, and Appendix II Assumption remains unresolved, so exhaustive item and form totals remain null.

extent.description: Fourteen source-structured DIV2 groups are described non-exhaustively. Raw L=115040 and LG=2591 repeat parallel versions and include editorial or nonverbal rows; the principal alignment through 29555 is an edition coordinate, not intrinsic work extent.

content_parts.label: Table of contents: Fairfax
content_parts.summary: Bodleian Library MS Fairfax 14. Unnumbered contents table.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=0, LG=0, PB=7; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Target witness component is Cursor Mundi in Fairfax 14; this is editorial table matter, not a separately counted work.

content_parts.label: Table of contents: Goettingen
content_parts.summary: SUB Goettingen 2 Cod. Ms. theol. 107r Cim.. Unnumbered contents table; source locus f. 1r.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=0, LG=0, PB=7; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Editorial table matter; not a separately counted work.

content_parts.label: Table of contents: Laud
content_parts.summary: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 416. Unnumbered contents table; surviving source leaf identified as f. 65b.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=0, LG=0, PB=2; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
One DIV3; edition describes this as the only leaf left.

content_parts.label: Main parallel version: Cotton Vespasian
content_parts.summary: British Library Cotton MS Vespasian A III, ff. 2-163. Principal edition alignment 1-29555.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=30043, LG=701, PB=841; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Raw L count includes additions and nonverbal/dotted rows and is not an intrinsic work-line total.

content_parts.label: Main parallel version: Fairfax
content_parts.summary: Bodleian Library MS Fairfax 14. Edition alignment begins at 1 and survives through printed label 27900.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=27549, LG=683, PB=787; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Milestones begin at f. 4a; DIMEV places core Cursor Mundi at ff. 4r-83r; later additions and Appendix IV continue in the codex; leaves after the catchword are wanting at this stream boundary.

content_parts.label: Main parallel version: Goettingen
content_parts.summary: SUB Goettingen 2 Cod. Ms. theol. 107r Cim.. Edition alignment 1-25766; manuscript target locus f. 1r-169v.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=25634, LG=598, PB=730; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Catalog subdivides prologue, seven main sections, additions, prayers, and Book of Penance; final quires are missing and the text breaks at 25766.

content_parts.label: Fourth serialized column: Trinity and RCPE
content_parts.summary: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.8 plus RCPE DEP/DRU/1. Trinity 1-21600; RCPE embedded extract 21601-21848; Trinity 21849-23944; RCPE main continuation 23945-24968.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=24361, LG=539, PB=696; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.

Last printed numeric label is 24965; three genuine unnumbered verbal lines extend the direct verse to 24968; two following dotted display rows are excluded. First printed RCPE f. 10v marker is at 23959. The embedded extract is the surviving fragment at the f. 45/f. 46 lacuna, not an invented continuous folio range.

content_parts.label: Cotton Galba continuation/right column
content_parts.summary: British Library Cotton MS Galba E IX. First ten opening lines align at 25103-25112; first numeric label is 25106; stream continues through 29555.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=1760, LG=23, PB=52; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Manuscript order differs at the opening. The printed gap 24969-25102 belongs to another parallel stream; Cotton Galba must not be represented as beginning at 24969.

content_parts.label: Appendix I: Edinburgh fragment
content_parts.summary: RCPE DEP/DRU/1, Hand C. Edition alignment 18989-23944 with lacunae.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=3513, LG=28, PB=50; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
DIMEV locus ff. 37r/a-50v/b; misbound and imperfect; two folios missing between ff. 45 and 46; XML jumps 21264 to 21849.

content_parts.label: Appendix II: Assumption
content_parts.summary: British Library Add MS 10036. Internal appendix numbering 1-904 plus final Latin colophon.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=905, LG=7, PB=12; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.

Source milestones run approximately f. 62b-f. 80b. DIMEV reports 784 lines for the Assumption item; preserve the 784 scholarly count versus 905 raw XML rows as unresolved rather than normalizing it away.

content_parts.label: Appendix III: Laud prologue
content_parts.summary: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 416. Direct positions 1-274; printed numeric labels through 270.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=274, LG=2, PB=8; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Source milestones center on ff. 66-67; final four verbal lines are unnumbered.

content_parts.label: Appendix III: Bedford/Add 36983 prologue and specimen
content_parts.summary: British Library Add MS 36983, formerly Bedford. Prologue 274 rows (labels through 270 plus four); specimen 234 rows aligned 9517-9752.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=508, LG=3, PB=13; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.

Terminal XML N=22004 on the fourth prologue-ending line is a witness-work endpoint label, not prologue sequence 274. DIMEV reports ff. 1r-148v and 154v-174v, omission of 14916-17288, and work endpoint 22004.

content_parts.label: Appendix III: Arundel prologue
content_parts.summary: College of Arms MS Arundel 57. Witness lacks positions 1-152; edition prints 153-270 (118 rows) plus two opening main-text lines.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=120, LG=2, PB=4; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Do not inflate this bounded prologue specimen into complete-work witness coverage.

content_parts.label: Appendix IV: Cato's Morals
content_parts.summary: Bodleian Library MS Fairfax 14, from f. 122. 336 main rows plus 37 epilogue rows; printed numbering reaches 378.
notes: Raw serialized metrics: L=373, LG=5, PB=5; these are representation metrics, not intrinsic item or verse totals.
Raw 373 versus printed 378 reflects skipped or anomalous numbering. This is a distinct intellectual item appended to the Cursor Mundi edition; the cached Fairfax f. 59v image does not cover it.

related_works.title: Cursor Mundi
notes: Counts once despite its parallel witness streams.

related_works.title: Cato's Morals (incomplete Fairfax version)

Links the scholarly occurrence data used for work identity, witness loci, genre/subject support, and unresolved named-unit boundaries.

open_questions.question: Can a narrower composition date or place be supported, and should any historical John of Lindbergh attribution be retained beyond an attribution-history note?
next_steps: Review exact modern scholarship; keep the creator anonymous and the place/date qualified meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Which Göttingen additions, prayers, Book of Penance units, and Appendix II Assumption should count as independent works in this compilation?
next_steps: Resolve each exact identity before replacing null exhaustive item/form totals.

open_questions.question: How should DIMEV's 784-line Assumption description be reconciled with 905 raw XML L rows and internal numbering 1-904 plus a Latin colophon?
next_steps: Compare the manuscript, DIMEV counting rules, Morris print, and XML row types without normalizing away the discrepancy.

open_questions.question: What date and more exact work/version identity can be assigned to the incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals appendix?
next_steps: Research the exact Fairfax Cato tradition and f. 122 onward before adding a separate composition date or narrower uniform title.

notes: Content structure is intentionally partial: sections=14 while exhaustive item, prose/verse item, verse-group, and verse-line totals remain null.
Seven original EETS issued parts, four U-M rebound carriers, and three Toronto scan tranches are distinct boundaries.
The fourth serialized witness stream switches Trinity to RCPE, back to Trinity, and back to RCPE at exact scoped line ranges.
No independent-work count is invented for the named additions, prayers, Assumption, or Penance.
No lineage relation asserts that Morris directly consulted a present physical codex.
